title: Europe’s warming accelerates beyond global trend, new Copernicus data shows
content: The report, published today by the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) and produced by the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF), shows Europe is the fastest-warming continent on Earth.
snippet: The report, published today by the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) and produced by the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF), shows Europe is the fastest-warming continent

title: Commission opens in-depth investigation into proposed joint venture between UPM and Sappi
content: The European Commission has opened an in-depth investigation to assess, under the EU Merger Regulation, the proposed creation of a joint venture between UPM-Kymmene Corporation ('UPM') and Sappi Limited ('Sappi').
snippet: The European Commission has opened an in-depth investigation to assess, under the EU Merger Regulation, the proposed creation of a joint venture between UPM-Kymmene Corporation ('UPM') and Sappi Limit

title: Main differences between the EES and ETIAS: What travellers need to know
content: Europe is changing its border management with the two new systems: the Entry/Exit System (EES) and the European Travel Information and Authorisation System (ETIAS). Both systems modernise and digitalise EU border checks, but they affect travellers coming from outside the EU in different ways.

title: Review highlights Digital Markets Act remains fit for purpose and has positive impact
content: The first review of the DMA found that the DMA remains fit for purpose. The findings show that the regulation has opened up new opportunities for businesses and developers, while giving users more choice and control over their experiences and devices.
snippet: The first review of the DMA found that the DMA remains fit for purpose. The findings show that the regulation has opened up new opportunities for businesses and developers, while giving users more cho

title: EU Member States saw a 27% drop in first-time asylum applications in 2025
content: A recent Eurostat publication revealed a 27% decrease in first-time asylum applications in the EU between 2024 and 2025. Five EU countries accounted for 83% of all first-time asylum applicants while first-time applications lodged by unaccompanied minors decreased by 38.9% in 2025.

title: How Can Municipalities Address Conflicts When Redesigning Streets?
content: The Conflictedstreets project explores the resistance to change that often occurs when streets are redesigned to be more pedestrian, cyclist and public transport friendly.
snippet: The Conflictedstreets project explores the resistance to change that often occurs when streets are redesigned to be more pedestrian, cyclist and public transport friendly.

title: Commission seeks feedback on measures to ensure interoperability with Google's Android under the Digital Markets Act
content: Commission seeks feedback on measures to ensure interoperability with Google's Android under the DMA
snippet: Commission seeks feedback on measures to ensure interoperability with Google's Android under the DMA

title: Youth Dialogue in Barcelona: Young voices help shape the EU’s next steps on housing
content: On Monday 20 April, Energy and Housing Commissioner Dan Jørgensen met with a group of young people for a Youth Dialogue on housing in Barcelona: a great opportunity for him to hear at first-hand how today’s housing pressures are reshaping study choices, careers, health and independence.

title: Solidarity Lanes: Latest figures – March 2026
content: Latest figures on Ukrainian exports and imports via the EU-Ukraine Solidarity Lanes: new transport routes established in the face of Russia’s war of aggression against Ukraine.
snippet: Latest figures on Ukrainian exports and imports via the EU-Ukraine Solidarity Lanes: new transport routes established in the face of Russia’s war of aggression against Ukraine.

title: Statement by the Commission and the High Representative ahead of the 40 years since the Chernobyl disaster
content: Forty years ago, the disaster at the Chernobyl Power Plant marked one of the gravest nuclear disasters in human history.
snippet: Forty years ago, the disaster at the Chernobyl Power Plant marked one of the gravest nuclear disasters in human history.

title: EU to strengthen regional dimension of research and innovation
content: The European Commission and the European Committee of the Regions signed a new Joint Action Plan to enhance the role of cities and regions in research and innovation (R&I).
snippet: The European Commission and the European Committee of the Regions signed a new Joint Action Plan to enhance the role of cities and regions in research and innovation (R&I).

title: Gas and Oil Coordination Groups take stock of situation on gas and oil markets
content: Experts from the European Commission, EU countries and the International Energy Agency (IEA) met in the Gas and Oil Coordination Groups today to discuss the EU’s gas and oil security of supply situation in Europe, as the Middle East conflict enters its third month.

title: Successful annual circular economy conference ends with crucial message
content: The European Circular Economy Stakeholder Platform's (ECESP) annual gathering redefined the circular economy as a vital economic and security strategy necessary for Europe's industrial resilience and resource independence.

title: EU and US launch strategic partnership on critical minerals
content: Today, the EU and US signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on a strategic partnership on critical minerals and agreed an EU-US Critical Minerals Action Plan.
snippet: Today, the EU and US signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on a strategic partnership on critical minerals and agreed an EU-US Critical Minerals Action Plan.

title: EU and EIB to boost businesses in partner countries through €1.3 billion financing
content: As a result of a new agreement between the European Commission and EIB Global, private project partners, small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and financial institutions in EU’s partner countries will have access to €1.3 billion in funding to support infrastructure projects in clean energy...

title: EU customs education partners meet in Tallinn to strengthen training collaboration
content: On 25–26 March 2026, representatives from European universities, customs administrations, and the European Commission gathered in Tallinn, Estonia, to discuss the future of customs education under the EU Recognition of Customs Academic Programmes initiative.

title: Applications open for 2026 Excellence in Road Safety Awards
content: Organisations and individuals making a demonstrable contribution to safer roads are encouraged to apply by 10 June 2026.
snippet: Organisations and individuals making a demonstrable contribution to safer roads are encouraged to apply by 10 June 2026.

title: Bilbao to host Cities Forum 2027
content: The 7th edition of the Cities Forum, the European Commission’s flagship event dedicated to the urban dimension of EU Cohesion Policy and co-organised with the European Urban Initiative, will take place in 2027 in Bilbao.

title: Commission seeks views on additional exemptions to portable batteries removability rules
content: The Commission launched a public consultation on new rules exempting more products from battery removability and replaceability rules.
snippet: The Commission launched a public consultation on new rules exempting more products from battery removability and replaceability rules.

title: New Generalised Scheme of Preferences approved for application in 2027
content: The European Commission welcomes today’s vote by the European Parliament in favour of the new Generalised Scheme of Preference (GSP), which represents the final step before its entry into application on 1 January 2027.
snippet: The European Commission welcomes today’s vote by the European Parliament in favour of the new Generalised Scheme of Preference (GSP), which represents the final step before its entry into application

title: Commission services and EDPB will start joint work on guidance on the interplay between EU competition law and data protection law
content: The Commission services and the European Data Protection Board (EDPB) have agreed to work together to clarify and give guidance on the interplay between EU competition law and data protection law.
snippet: The Commission services and the European Data Protection Board (EDPB) have agreed to work together to clarify and give guidance on the interplay between EU competition law and data protection law.

title: Schengen Information System: Europe’s key security and border database helps rescue missing minors and arrest cross-border criminals
content: The Schengen Information System is the largest information-sharing system for security and border management in Europe. Its key successes include the arrests of dangerous cross-border criminals, the apprehension of terrorists detected in the EU, and reuniting missing children with their families.

title: Driving sustainable farming: new guidance for CAP 2028–2034
content: The Commission publishes best practices to help Member States design effective agri-environmental and climate actions under the future CAP, boosting sustainability and farmer participation across the EU.

title: New study on fishing fleet capacity and sustainability
content: Today, the European Commission published a study assessing the state of the EU fishing fleet, including its evolution, the challenges it currently faces and its future sustainability.
snippet: Today, the European Commission published a study assessing the state of the EU fishing fleet, including its evolution, the challenges it currently faces and its future sustainability.

title: Notification by Germany of voluntary cancellation for plants closed in 2024
content: On 20 November 2025, the Commission received a notification in the sense of Article 12(4) of Directive 2003/87/EC (‘ETS Directive’) from Germany.
snippet: On 20 November 2025, the Commission received a notification in the sense of Article 12(4) of Directive 2003/87/EC (‘ETS Directive’) from Germany.

title: Assessing climate change risks along Spain’s coast
content: A new project aims to assess the risks that climate change pose to the development of marine aquaculture along the entire Spanish coastline.
snippet: A new project aims to assess the risks that climate change pose to the development of marine aquaculture along the entire Spanish coastline.

title: Global Gateway: Commissioner Síkela announces new investments on his mission to Addis Ababa
content: During his two-day mission to Addis Ababa, Commissioner Jozef Síkela opened the EU-Ethiopia Business Forum and announced a new major investments package. This will strengthen the partnership between the European Union and Ethiopia and advance the European Global Gateway investments strategy.

title: Provisional Application of the EU-Mercosur Interim Trade Agreement Begins 1 May 2026
content: On 1 May 2026, the EU-Mercosur Interim Trade Agreement (ITA) will enter into provisional application.
snippet: On 1 May 2026, the EU-Mercosur Interim Trade Agreement (ITA) will enter into provisional application.

title: Safeguarding Chornobyl: strengthening protection and resilience in Ukraine
content: Nearly four decades after the disaster, the site is once again at the centre of concern amid ongoing instability in Ukraine due to the on-going armed conflict.
snippet: Nearly four decades after the disaster, the site is once again at the centre of concern amid ongoing instability in Ukraine due to the on-going armed conflict.

title: Institution Building Days 2026: Strengthening Institutions and Improving Lives

title: Chornobyl 40 years later: Learning from the past
content: How we manage nuclear safety and prepare for the future, must be guided by the lessons we learn from the past. 40 years since the Chernobyl disaster, take a look at what the EU is doing to promote nuclear safety around the world and how it has helped to protect Chernobyl from further leakage.
